content: I want to understand 3D Gaussian Splatting end to end. Start with the Kerbl 2023 paper.

reasoning: Pull the canonical 3DGS page, then walk the dependency graph backward to NeRF/MVS prerequisites and forward to follow-ons.
content: I'll pull the canonical 3DGS paper, then trace its prerequisites and direct successors via the graph.

content.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering
content.body: Kerbl et al. introduce 3D GaussianSplatting, a primitive-based representation for novel-view synthesis that recovers a scene as a collection of anisotropic 3D Gaussians and renders them by differentiable rasterisation. Each Gaussian carries a position, a full 3x3 covariance, an opacity, and a view-dependent colour encoded as spherical harmonics. The optimisation is seeded from the sparse PointCloud produced by a standard StructureFromMotion pass; an interleaved densification and pruning schedule clones, splits, and removes primitives during training; a fast tile rasteriser keeps the backward pass closed-form. >=30 FPS at 1080p on unbounded scenes.

reasoning: Two directions: backward to NeRF/SfM, forward through SuGaR/2DGS/Scaffold-GS/MonoGS/4DGS/Revising. Read NeRF first to establish the prerequisite.
content: Clean dependency graph. NeRF is the only upstream paper edge. Let me read it next.

content.title: NeRF: Representing Scenes as Neural Radiance Fields for View Synthesis
content.body: Mildenhall et al. introduce Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF): a 5D coordinate-MLP that maps (x, y, z, theta, phi) to a volume density and view-dependent radiance, with novel views synthesised by querying along camera rays and compositing via classical volume rendering. Positional encoding and a hierarchical coarse-to-fine sampling scheme make it work. Per-scene, photometric loss only, no explicit geometry supervision.

content.title: SuGaR: Surface-Aligned Gaussian Splatting
content.body: Guedon and Lepetit close 3DGS's mesh-extraction gap with a regulariser that aligns Gaussians to the underlying scene surface, then runs Poisson reconstruction on the oriented centres to produce a watertight mesh in minutes. An optional refinement stage binds Gaussians to the mesh by barycentric coordinate so the mesh provides edit handles.

content.title: 2D Gaussian Splatting
content.body: Huang et al. collapse each volumetric primitive to a flat oriented disk so the implied geometry is view-consistent. Perspective-correct rasteriser; depth-distortion and normal-consistency regularisers.

content.title: Gaussian Splatting SLAM
content.body: Matsuki et al. (MonoGS) use 3DGS as the sole map representation in a monocular SLAM loop. Camera tracking by differentiating the splat render w.r.t. pose. About 3 FPS. Handles RGB-D when available without architectural change.

content.title: 4D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Dynamic Scene Rendering
content.body: Wu et al. extend 3DGS to dynamic scenes by attaching a deformation field (HexPlane-style spatio-temporal feature) to each canonical Gaussian. Renders dynamic scenes in real time; struggles past short clips.

content.title: Mathematical Supplement for the gsplat Library
content.body: Ye et al. derive the closed-form forward and backward passes of the tile-based splatting rasteriser used by the gsplat reference implementation (nerfstudio-project/gsplat). The supplement is the canonical reference for the gradient of the rendered colour with respect to each Gaussian's mean, covariance, opacity, and spherical-harmonic coefficients.

content.title: Scaffold-GS: Structured 3D Gaussians for View-Adaptive Rendering
content.body: Lu et al. replace the free Gaussian soup with anchor points on a regular grid; each anchor spawns a small set of view-dependent offsets and attributes via a tiny MLP. Lower primitive count, better low-frequency regions.

reasoning: Eight papers in hand plus NeRF prerequisite. Lay them out as a dependency tree: NeRF -> 3DGS -> {SuGaR, 2DGS, Scaffold-GS, MonoGS, 4DGS, Revising, gsplat-impl}.
content:
Here is the end-to-end lineage:

  Prerequisite
    NeRF (Mildenhall 2020, arxiv-2003-08934)
        - establishes the differentiable volume-rendering paradigm and per-scene photometric optimisation that 3DGS replaces with primitives.

  Anchor
    3D Gaussian Splatting (Kerbl 2023, arxiv-2308-04079)
        - swaps NeRF's ray-marched MLP for a tile-rasterised cloud of anisotropic 3D Gaussians seeded from SfM. >=30 FPS at 1080p.

  Implementation reference
    gsplat math supplement (Ye 2023, arxiv-2312-02121)
        - derives the closed-form forward / backward of the tile rasteriser; canonical reference for any GS gradient question.

  Geometry branch
    SuGaR (Guedon 2023, arxiv-2311-12775) -> mesh from a regularised Gaussian field.
    2D Gaussian Splatting (Huang 2024, arxiv-2403-17888) -> view-consistent geometry by construction.

  Structure branch
    Scaffold-GS (Lu 2023, arxiv-2312-00109) -> anchor-based replacement for the free soup.
    Revising Densification (Bulo 2024, arxiv-2404-06109) -> corrects the clone/split/prune schedule downstream methods inherit.

  SLAM branch
    Gaussian Splatting SLAM / MonoGS (Matsuki 2023, arxiv-2312-06741) -> 3DGS as the sole map representation under live monocular capture.

  Dynamic branch
    4D Gaussian Splatting (Wu 2023, arxiv-2310-08528) -> deformation field attached to each canonical Gaussian.

Read in that order, the original paper plus the seven downstream nodes cover the representation, its math, its surface story, its structure story, its SLAM story, and its dynamic story. If you want code to run alongside the reading, the `graphdeco-inria/gaussian-splatting` repo is the reference and `nerfstudio-project/gsplat` is the cleaner library implementation.
